channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Microsoft Teams to retire Together mode and prioritize Gallery for better video quality
item.description: Microsoft Teams will officially retire its Together mode on June 30, marking nearly six years since its introduction in July 2020. Together mode was designed to create a more immersive virtual meeting...

item.title: Pep Guardiola Congratulates Man City U18s After FA Youth Cup Win Over Manchester United
item.description: Manchester City Under-18s claimed a 2-1 victory over Manchester United in the FA Youth Cup final, marking their fifth triumph in the competition. The match, held at Joie Stadium, saw City take the lea...

item.title: Hydro-Québec resisted sharing historical records with Newfoundland government
item.description: Documents from the 1960s reveal that Hydro-Québec actively resisted sharing certain records with the Newfoundland government. The documents, uncovered decades later, suggest the utility company took d...

item.title: Exploring the Mundanity and Absurdity of Corporate Offices Through Lars Tunbjörk’s Photography
item.description: Lars Tunbjörk’s iconic photobook, Office / LA Office, recently reissued in an expanded edition by Loose Joints, offers a darkly humorous and critical look at corporate office life. Capturing offices i...

item.title: CIA Director Travels to Cuba Amid US Calls for Policy Changes
item.description: The Director of the CIA recently visited Cuba in a high-profile diplomatic engagement, signaling a continuation of complex U.S.-Cuba relations. This visit comes amid heightened American demands for su...

item.title: Political Cartoonists Summarize the Week's Events in U.S. Politics
item.description: This Politico gallery showcases the best political cartoons from across the United States for the week of May 14, 2026. Edited by Matt Wuerker, the collection features works from cartoonists spanning ...

item.title: Global stock markets fall following record levels in South Korea amid U.S.-China diplomatic visit
item.description: Global financial markets experienced a downturn as South Korea's Kospi index reached record highs, reflecting investor caution amid ongoing international developments. The drop comes as former U.S. Pr...

item.title: Tanzania Accelerates Digital Customs Reforms to Boost Trade Efficiency
item.description: Tanzania, under Finance Minister Khamis Mussa Omar, is moving fast on digital customs reforms to improve trade flows and regional cooperation. The minister highlighted that global trade is changing qu...

item.title: Fox News host criticized for offensive comments about Black representation in Congress
item.description: Fox News host Jesse Watters faced widespread condemnation after making highly offensive remarks about Black voters during a segment on 'The Five.' Watters argued that Black Americans do not have enoug...

item.title: The Devil Wears Prada 2: A Reflection on the Decline of Journalism
item.description: The sequel to The Devil Wears Prada goes beyond fashion and style to explore the quiet collapse of the journalism industry. The film follows Andy Sachs, now experienced and world-weary, as she and her...

item.title: Florence Fleming Noyes’ Students Practice Free Dance in Central Park, 1916
item.description: This historic photograph, taken by Franz Otto Koch in January 1916 in New York's Central Park, captures students of the Noyes School of Rhythm dancing barefoot in the snow. Led by Florence Fleming Noy...

item.title: Trent University Study Highlights the Challenges of AI Use in Classrooms
item.description: A recent study from Trent University reveals that the widespread use of artificial intelligence among Canadian post-secondary students is creating significant challenges for educators. Professors repo...

item.title: Train Collides with School Bus in Gilgil, Pupils Injured
item.description: In Gilgil, Nakuru County, a school trip turned into a shocking incident on Friday, May 15, when a train collided with a school bus belonging to Teresia’s School at a railway crossing along the Gilgil–...

item.title: DOJ sets up committee to investigate Senate shooting incident
item.description: The Department of Justice (DOJ) has officially formed a special panel to investigate the recent gunfire incident that occurred at the Senate. The move comes as authorities aim to thoroughly examine th...

item.title: Hisbah arrests six people, including married woman, during hotel raid in Kebbi
item.description: For Kebbi State, officers of the Hisbah Board, wey dey operate under Ministry of Religious Affairs, don arrest six people after dem carry out one raid for hotel wey dey behind Presidential Lodge for B...

item.title: Education Minister explains NEET-UG 2026 issues and plans computer-based exams from next year
item.description: The NEET-UG 2026 examination has faced significant controversy due to technical issues experienced during the OMR-based test. According to the Education Minister, the main reason behind the disruption...

item.title: Xi-Trump summit highlights potential for US-China cooperation amid Taiwan tensions
item.description: The recent summit in Beijing between US President Donald Trump and Chinese President Xi Jinping sought to address trade, technology, and regional security concerns, but the issue of Taiwan remained th...

item.title: Trump Considers Mass Pardons Amidst Birthday and Political Distractions
item.description: The article discusses speculation around U.S. President Donald Trump potentially issuing 250 pardons on June 14, 2026, coinciding with both Flag Day and his 80th birthday. The author argues that the t...

item.title: Defense Secretary Cancels Planned U.S. Troop Deployment to Poland, Raising European Security Concerns
item.description: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth unexpectedly canceled a planned deployment of 4,000 U.S. troops to Poland, catching both Pentagon officials and European allies by surprise. Troops and equipment from Te...

item.title: Moody’s Confirms Tanzania’s Stable B1 Rating as Key Sectors Drive Growth
item.description: Moody’s Ratings has confirmed Tanzania’s long-term issuer rating at B1 with a stable outlook, showing confidence in the country’s economy despite political turbulence in 2025. The economy is projected...
